steps.description: Click the "Declarative Agent" option in the "New Project" dropdown within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension in Visual Studio Code.

Click the "Start with TypeSpec for Microsoft 365 Copilot" option in the dropdown menu labeled "Add an action to your declarative agent" within the "Create Declarative Agent" dialog in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Default folder" option in the folder selection dropdown within the Workspace Folder prompt in Visual Studio Code (Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface).

parameters.sample:
=== Generated Script ===
Language: python

```python
import os
file_path = "/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/vscuse_app_QUTvT/src/agent/main.tsp"

if not os.path.isfile(file_path):
    raise FileNotFoundError(f"File not found: {file_path}")

with open(file_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    lines = f.readlines()

# Uncomment lines 17-20 and line 23 (lines are 0-indexed in Python)
for idx in list(range(16,20)) + [22]:
    if lines[idx].lstrip().startswith("//"):
        # Remove leading '//' to uncomment
        lines[idx] = lines[idx].replace('//', '', 1)

with open(file_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    f.writelines(lines)

print("Uncommented lines 17-20 and line 23 successfully.")
```

=== Execution Results ===
Exit Code: 0

=== STDOUT ===
Uncommented lines 17-20 and line 23 successfully.

✓ Script executed successfully

steps.description: @code In the file content: "/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/${{var:app_name}}/src/agent/main.tsp", uncomment line 17-20 and line 23. then save the file.
Click the "Provision" button under the "LIFECYCLE" section in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit pane within Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Install" button in the extension recommendation popup for TypeSpec at the bottom-right of the Visual Studio Code window.

@assertion there's notification message include: "provision stage executed successfully". 
And no error message exist.
Press Ctrl+Shift+P to open the Command Palette in Visual Studio Code with the "MICROSOFT 365 AGENTS TOOLKIT" panel active.
Type 'notifications: clear' into the Visual Studio Code Command Palette input to filter and select the "Notifications: Clear All Notifications" command.
Press Enter to select "Notifications: Clear All Notifications" from the command palette in Visual Studio Code.
Press Ctrl+J in Visual Studio Code to toggle the OUTPUT panel displaying Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit logs.
Click inside the "Message Copilot" text input field in the M365 Copilot chat interface for the agent "vscuseappyccHG" to focus the cursor for typing.
Type "get latest issues" into the chat input box within the vscuseappyccHG agent conversation in the M365 Copilot web interface.
Press Enter in the chat input box containing the text "get latest issues" within the vscuseappyccHG agent conversation in the M365 Copilot web interface.
Click the "Always" permission button below the GitHub connection prompt in the M365 Copilot chat interface to allow ongoing access.
@assertion there's reply message include github issues.
